title: Four-Wheel Steering — Rear Servo Actuator
design_question: How to implement active four-wheel steering with speed-dependent rear steer angle for improved manoeuvrability and stability?
selected_approach: Front steering: conventional rack and pinion. Rear steering: 12 V DC servo actuator (200 N, 10 mm/s) moves rear track rod. Arduino Mega reads front steering angle (potentiometer on rack) and vehicle speed (hall-effect sensor). Rear steer angle δ_r = k(v)·δ_f: k = −0.3 at v < 30 km/h (counter-phase), k = +0.1 at v > 60 km/h (in-phase). Linear interpolation between 30–60 km/h. Fail-safe: spring returns rear to 0° on power loss.
rationale: Counter-phase rear steer reduces turning radius 25–30% at parking speeds. In-phase at highway speeds reduces yaw response overshoot. Spring fail-safe ensures straight-line stability on actuator failure. Arduino implementation is straightforward.
verification_notes: FACT: 'Ackermann Four-Wheel Steering Geometry' gives complete geometry analysis and δ_r = k·δ_f relationship.
supporting_fact_titles: DC Gear Motor with PWM Speed Control
